org.apache.flink.table.connector.sink

## Interface DynamicTableSink

  * All Known Implementing Classes:
    [HBaseDynamicTableSink](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/hbase/sink/HBaseDynamicTableSink.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.hbase.sink"), [JdbcDynamicTableSink](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/jdbc/table/JdbcDynamicTableSink.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.jdbc.table"), [Kafka010DynamicSink](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/connectors/kafka/table/Kafka010DynamicSink.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.connectors.kafka.table"), [Kafka011DynamicSink](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/connectors/kafka/table/Kafka011DynamicSink.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.connectors.kafka.table"), [KafkaDynamicSink](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/connectors/kafka/table/KafkaDynamicSink.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.connectors.kafka.table"), [KafkaDynamicSinkBase](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/connectors/kafka/table/KafkaDynamicSinkBase.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.connectors.kafka.table")

* * *




        [@PublicEvolving](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/annotation/PublicEvolving.html> "annotation in org.apache.flink.annotation")
        public interface DynamicTableSink

Sink of a dynamic table to an external storage system.

Dynamic tables are the core concept of Flink's Table & SQL API for processing both bounded and unbounded data in a unified fashion. By definition, a dynamic table can change over time.

When writing a dynamic table, the content can always be considered as a changelog (finite or infinite) for which all changes are written out continuously until the changelog is exhausted. The given [`ChangelogMode`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/connector/ChangelogMode.html> "class in org.apache.flink.table.connector") indicates the set of changes that the sink accepts during runtime.

For regular batch scenarios, the sink can solely accept insert-only rows and write out bounded streams.

For regular streaming scenarios, the sink can solely accept insert-only rows and can write out unbounded streams.

For change data capture (CDC) scenarios, the sink can write out bounded or unbounded streams with insert, update, and delete rows. See also [`RowKind`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/types/RowKind.html> "enum in org.apache.flink.types").

Instances of [`DynamicTableSink`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/connector/sink/DynamicTableSink.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.table.connector.sink") can be seen as factories that eventually produce concrete runtime implementation for writing the actual data.

Depending on the optionally declared abilities, the planner might apply changes to an instance and thus mutate the produced runtime implementation.

A [`DynamicTableSink`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/connector/sink/DynamicTableSink.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.table.connector.sink") can implement the following abilities:

    * [`SupportsPartitioning`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/connector/sink/abilities/SupportsPartitioning.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.table.connector.sink.abilities")
    * [`SupportsOverwrite`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/connector/sink/abilities/SupportsOverwrite.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.table.connector.sink.abilities")

In the last step, the planner will call [`getSinkRuntimeProvider(Context)`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/connector/sink/DynamicTableSink.html#getSinkRuntimeProvider-org.apache.flink.table.connector.sink.DynamicTableSink.Context->) for obtaining a provider of runtime implementation.

  *     * ### Method Detail

      * #### getChangelogMode

            [ChangelogMode](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/connector/ChangelogMode.html> "class in org.apache.flink.table.connector") getChangelogMode([ChangelogMode](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/connector/ChangelogMode.html> "class in org.apache.flink.table.connector") requestedMode)

Returns the set of changes that the sink accepts during runtime.

The planner can make suggestions but the sink has the final decision what it requires. If the planner does not support this mode, it will throw an error. For example, the sink can return that it only supports [`ChangelogMode.insertOnly()`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/connector/ChangelogMode.html#insertOnly-->).

Parameters:
    `requestedMode` \- expected set of changes by the current plan
      * #### getSinkRuntimeProvider

            [DynamicTableSink.SinkRuntimeProvider](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/connector/sink/DynamicTableSink.SinkRuntimeProvider.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.table.connector.sink") getSinkRuntimeProvider([DynamicTableSink.Context](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/connector/sink/DynamicTableSink.Context.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.table.connector.sink") context)

Returns a provider of runtime implementation for writing the data.

There might exist different interfaces for runtime implementation which is why [`DynamicTableSink.SinkRuntimeProvider`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/connector/sink/DynamicTableSink.SinkRuntimeProvider.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.table.connector.sink") serves as the base interface. Concrete [`DynamicTableSink.SinkRuntimeProvider`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/connector/sink/DynamicTableSink.SinkRuntimeProvider.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.table.connector.sink") interfaces might be located in other Flink modules.

Independent of the provider interface, the table runtime expects that a sink implementation accepts internal data structures (see [`RowData`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/data/RowData.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.table.data") for more information).

The given [`DynamicTableSink.Context`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/connector/sink/DynamicTableSink.Context.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.table.connector.sink") offers utilities by the planner for creating runtime implementation with minimal dependencies to internal data structures.

See `org.apache.flink.table.connector.sink.SinkFunctionProvider` in `flink-table-api-java-bridge`.

      * #### copy

            [DynamicTableSink](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/connector/sink/DynamicTableSink.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.table.connector.sink") copy()

Creates a copy of this instance during planning. The copy should be a deep copy of all mutable members.

      * #### asSummaryString

            [String](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/String.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang") asSummaryString()

Returns a string that summarizes this sink for printing to a console or log.
